title: First Paycheck
narration: I remember holding that first check like it was a diploma. $187.42 after taxes. I worked a whole week at the car wash for that. My hands were raw from scrubbing rims in the sun. But when I saw my name printed on that check, I felt like I owned something for the first time. I didn't spend it right away. I just kept looking at it. Then I took my mom to dinner at the place she always drove past but never walked into. She ordered without looking at the price. That was worth more than any amount.

scenes.text: I remember holding that first check like it was a diploma.
